summary: The author secured 2nd place by fine-tuning Gemma-2 and Llama-3 models initialized from a previous competition's efficient framework. A core strategy involved iterative pseudo-labeling using external datasets and large open-source models, progressively expanding non-English data to boost multilingual performance. The pipeline combined careful data truncation, soft-label averaging, and a two-stage training schedule (English-first, then multilingual) to mitigate noise. Final inference leveraged Test-Time Augmentation with a weighted ensemble of swapped prediction heads, achieving a private score of 0.716.
extensive_summary:
## Code Framework & Base Models
The solution builds on @tascj0's efficient full-parameter fine-tuning and inference framework. Base models `gemma2-9b` and `ArmoRM-Llama3-8B-v0.1` are initialized from @tascj0's checkpoints rather than training from scratch. The original 3-class classification head is replaced with a 2-class head, yielding `lmsys-gemma-pretrain` and `lmsys-llama-pretrain`.

## Data Processing & Truncation
Input sequences (`prompt`, `response_a`, `response_b`) are proportionally truncated in the middle based on length ratios to preserve context while managing context windows.

## Iterative Pseudo-Labeling & Data Expansion
The author implements a multi-stage pseudo-labeling pipeline. Initially, soft labels are averaged from three models to create `hf-21k` using @nbroad's v1 and v2 datasets (v3 is excluded due to minimal model capability gaps causing noise). To improve multilingual robustness, the pipeline expands to `vllm-74k` derived from `lmsys-chat-1m` (deduplicated and filtered). Response pairs are generated via vLLM inference using a diverse set of open models (e.g., `Qwen/Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct`, `Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct`, `DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B`). The dataset is split by language and sampled into v1/v2/v3 subsets with progressively higher non-English ratios. Pseudo-labels are filtered by confidence (`abs(winner_model_a - winner_model_b) <= 0.03`).

## Training Strategy
Models undergo expanded pretraining on 95k combined pseudo-labels to reduce noise, followed by full fine-tuning on the WSDM dataset. A crucial scheduling choice splits training into two epochs: the first on a 90% English subset, and the second on a 10% English + multilingual subset.

## Inference & Ensembling
Final inference uses Test-Time Augmentation (TTA) with a weighted ensemble. `gemma-final` predicts PAB, while `llama-final` predicts PBA (swapped) only for high-confidence samples (`abs(winner_model_a - winner_model_b) < 0.8`). The predictions are weighted 2.5:1, improving the private score to 0.716.
competition_flow: Raw text data is truncated proportionally in the middle, then used to fine-tune Gemma-2 and Llama-3 models initialized from previous checkpoints; iterative pseudo-labeling with external datasets and large open models expands the training pool, followed by a two-epoch training schedule (English-first, then multilingual) and weighted TTA inference to produce the final submission.
data_reading: Text sequences (`prompt`, `response_a`, `response_b`) are loaded and proportionally truncated in the middle based on length ratios.
data_processing: Proportional middle truncation of `prompt`, `response_a`, and `response_b` based on length ratios
Deduplication of prompts from `lmsys-chat-1m` and removal of prompts overlapping with external datasets
Filtering pseudo-labels by confidence margin (`abs(winner_model_a - winner_model_b) <= 0.03`)
Sampling datasets with increasing non-English ratios to improve multilingual performance
Removal of 'think' sections from DeepSeek-R1 model outputs
cv_strategy: 80/20 train/validation split for initial fine-tuning, later expanded to 100% training data for final stages.
ensembling: Weighted ensemble of TTA predictions from `gemma-final` (PAB) and `llama-final` (PBA swapped) at a 2.5:1 ratio, with `llama-final` only applied to high-confidence samples (`abs(winner_model_a - winner_model_b) < 0.8`).
insights: Increasing the non-English data ratio in pseudo-labeling datasets significantly improves multilingual performance.
A two-epoch training schedule (English-first, then multilingual) effectively balances language capabilities without degrading English performance.
Filtering pseudo-labels by a confidence margin reduces noise during iterative training.
critical_findings: Excluding @nbroad's v3 pseudo-label dataset prevented noise because the model capability gaps were too minimal.
Using `llama-final` only for high-confidence predictions (`< 0.8` margin) during TTA improved the private score without hurting the LB score.
what_did_not_work: Qwen2.5-72B distillation (limited by time/GPU, lacked pretraining cycle)
